commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=676aeb5cea8475ca28c92645edc926fed472ef57)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a cron preflight warning when an `agentTurn` payload allowlists `web_search` and `listWebSearchProviders()` returns no managed search providers.

PR surface: Source +17. Total +17 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main can construct `web_search` when search is enabled and only reports missing providers if the tool executes. I did not run a live cron job in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor or maintainer updates for the native-search-aware warning condition plus real behavior proof; automation cannot supply the contributor's real setup proof.

target.title: fix(cron): warn when web_search is in toolsAllow but no search provider is enabled
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

When a cron job has `web_search` in its `toolsAllow` but every search provider plugin is disabled, the model delivers an apology ("I'm sorry, I can't search...") and the run completes with `status: ok`. No diagnostic, no warning, no signal to the operator.

Fixes #97654

## Why This Change Was Made

The cron preflight function `prepareCronRunContext` already performs model selection, model provider reachability, and thinking level validation preflights. However, there was no check for whether `toolsAllow` entries correspond to actually available providers.

This adds a preflight check that calls `listWebSearchProviders()` when `web_search` appears in the normalized `toolsAllow`. If no providers are registered (all search plugins disabled), a warning is logged directing the operator to enable a search plugin.

## Evidence

### Before
```
# Cron job with toolsAllow: ["web_search"], all search plugins disabled
$ openclaw cron run <job>
# → cron_run_logs shows status: ok, delivered: true
# → Content is model apology: "I'm unable to perform the web search..."
# → No warning anywhere
```

### After
```
# Same scenario — the gateway log now shows:
[cron:<id>] web_search is in toolsAllow but no search provider plugin is enabled.
Enable one with: openclaw plugins enable duckduckgo
```

### Test Results
```
✓ src/cron/isolated-agent/run.tools-allow.test.ts    3 passed
✓ src/cron/isolated-agent/run.payload-fallbacks.test.ts 6 passed
✓ src/cron/run-diagnostics.test.ts                   10 passed
```

## Merge Risk

Low. The change is a read-only preflight check that logs a warning. It does not alter any execution path, return value, or tool behavior. The import of `listWebSearchProviders` is the only new dependency.
target.author: krissding
